axis: TARGETARCH-conditional COPY (multi-stage scratch bridge) + tag GC — eliminate wrong-arch ELF waste from runtime image and prune session tag clutter

trigger.root_cause: Dockerfile previously COPY'd all four ELF pairs (cli + interp × arm64 + x86_64) into /tmp, then the per-arch RUN install step deleted the wrong pair. Layer-based file systems retain the COPY layer bytes regardless of subsequent rm — so each arch image carried ~2.3MB of unreachable wrong-arch ELF.

before.wrong_arch_layer_bytes_per_image: 537kB (cli x86_64 on arm64 image) + 2.1MB (interp x86_64 on arm64 image) ≈ 2.6MB

targetarch_copy_change.approach: multi-stage scratch bridge with FROM-alias indirection
dockerfile_diff_summary: ADD: two FROM scratch AS elf-arm64 / elf-amd64 stages, each COPY'ing only its own arch's pair into normalized /cli + /interp paths.
ADD: FROM elf-${TARGETARCH} AS elf-selected — alias stage that resolves at parse time via buildx's pre-defined TARGETARCH platform-arg.

REPLACE: the four COPY build/hexa_*_<arch> /tmp/... lines + the case-on-TARGETARCH RUN, with two COPY --from=elf-selected /cli|/interp /tmp/hexa_cli|/tmp/hexa_interp + a single arch-agnostic install RUN.

buildx_quirk_encountered.problem_1: COPY --from=elf-${TARGETARCH} did not interpolate at parse time — buildkit treated literal 'elf-${TARGETARCH}' as a Docker Hub image name (error: 'invalid reference format: repository name (library/elf-${TARGETARCH}) must be lowercase'). Confirmed: ARG-in-stage-name is NOT supported for `COPY --from=`.
buildx_quirk_encountered.problem_2: Adding `ARG TARGETARCH` at global scope (before any FROM) collapsed TARGETARCH to empty string in `FROM elf-${TARGETARCH}` (error: 'failed to parse stage name elf-'). The platform-arg auto-injection is overridden when the ARG is explicitly redeclared globally with no default.
buildx_quirk_encountered.fix: Remove the global `ARG TARGETARCH` declaration entirely. Use buildx's pre-defined TARGETARCH platform-arg directly in `FROM elf-${TARGETARCH} AS elf-selected`. Keep `ARG TARGETARCH` inside the runtime stage only (for downstream RUN visibility, even though no longer needed for the COPY now that --from references the alias).
buildx_quirk_encountered.lesson: Platform-args (TARGETOS, TARGETARCH, TARGETPLATFORM, BUILDPLATFORM, etc.) are pre-defined globally by buildx and visible in FROM stage names without explicit ARG declaration — but ARG-redeclaration at global scope WIPES the platform-arg with the user-supplied default (empty).

kept: hexa-runner:f9ccfa3d (immediate prior, ω-runner-4 lineage at 170MB / 37.2MB — rollback candidate; matches the live hexa-exec container's image cc74eea6c8cf)
deleted: hexa-runner:4e711d78 (image e709e02a2cce deleted — runner-4 build, superseded by f9ccfa3d)
hexa-runner:87916b4c (image de29583a8a9a deleted — pre-perl-removal experiment at 152MB)
hexa-runner:e075b3e8 (image fe167ea7a358 deleted — required removing stale exited container 50d64287e685 'happy_almeida' first)
hexa-runner:v5-test (image 9a4541a7c55a deleted — stale alias of pre-runner-1 latest)
tag_gc.note: Most deleted tags shared base layers with the current image (debian:12-slim 108MB, apt RUN 17.5MB), so layer-dedup means actual reclaimed bytes are modest (~140MB from unique layers in 87916b4c, ab8bec13, 5bd9f630/e075b3e8). Primary win is tag-list clarity (11 → 3) — reduces operator confusion during rollback.

delta.interpretation: 1.0MB compact save matches expected wrong-arch ELF removal (537kB cli + 2.1MB interp = 2.6MB raw, but layer compression on the install_run RUN step accounts for ~1.6MB of that). The dominant mass remaining is debian:12-slim base (108MB) + apt layer (17.5MB) — no further wins available without distroless/static-base migration.

test_2_run_script.verdict: PASS — no shasum/perl noise, tini PID-1 reaping working

test_3_e2e_arithmetic.verdict: PASS — interp path operative, len()/str()/index/while all functional

test_4_hot_container_unaffected.verdict: PASS — live hexa-exec container (cc74eea6c8cf / f9ccfa3d) still functional; rollback path intact

rollback.primary_path: docker tag hexa-runner:f9ccfa3d hexa-runner:latest && docker rm -f hexa-exec (resolver auto-spawns new container on next call from image cc74eea6c8cf)
rollback.secondary_path: git diff HEAD docker/runner/Dockerfile (Dockerfile change uncommitted per session policy — `git checkout -- docker/runner/Dockerfile` reverts source); rebuild via `hexa tool/build_runner_image.hexa`
rollback.buildx_state: hexa-runner-builder buildx instance retained; can rebuild any prior tag if Dockerfile reverted. Build cache holds intermediate layers from this cycle (free disk if needed: docker buildx prune -f --builder hexa-runner-builder).

next_cycles_seeded.name: distroless
next_cycles_seeded.rationale: Largest remaining mass is debian:12-slim base (108MB virtual). Migrating to gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 (~2MB) or a static musl scratch base would drop virtual size from 166MB → ~25MB. Trade-off: lose curl/wget — bridges that exec('curl …') would break. Mitigation: vendor a minimal curl static binary (~1MB) into the image, or replace curl-based bridges with a hexa-native HTTPS client (deferred to bridge cycle). Estimated saving: 100MB+ virtual.
next_cycles_seeded.blast_radius: high — affects 7+ bridges (oeis/gw/arxiv/simbad/wikipedia/openalex/uniprot per Dockerfile comment); needs a full bridge_health pass to validate

next_cycles_seeded.name: warm-exec benchmark
next_cycles_seeded.rationale: Measure cold vs warm container exec latency: `docker run --rm hexa-runner:latest run /work/foo.hexa` (cold spawn ~600ms-1s) vs `docker exec hexa-exec hexa run /work/foo.hexa` (warm ~50-200ms). Quantify the resolver's hot-container reuse benefit so future capacity planning can reason about per-call overhead.
next_cycles_seeded.blast_radius: low — pure measurement, no Dockerfile/code change

next_cycles_seeded.name: cgroup self-introspection
next_cycles_seeded.rationale: Bake a tool in the image (or extend self/main.hexa) that reads /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max + cpu.max + cpu.stat at startup, so workloads can self-report their resource ceiling. Useful for deterministic OOM diagnosis (vs. the current 'SIGKILL with no obvious cause' failure mode). Also enables cgroup-aware self-throttling.
next_cycles_seeded.blast_radius: low — additive read-only probe; no behaviour change

next_cycles_seeded.name: image-strip + debug-variant split
next_cycles_seeded.rationale: Current arm64 ELFs are built `with debug_info, not stripped` (per `file` output in build helper) — the `not stripped` flag suggests symbol tables present in the shipped binary. `strip --strip-unneeded` on the Linux ELFs before COPY could shave 100-300kB per binary (2 binaries × 2 arches = ~600kB-1.2MB). Pair with a separate hexa-runner:debug tag that retains symbols for post-mortem.
next_cycles_seeded.blast_radius: low — behaviour-preserving strip; verify with smoke after

next_cycles_seeded.name: tag GC automation
next_cycles_seeded.rationale: This cycle GC'd 8 tags manually. A `hexa tool/gc_runner_tags.hexa --keep=2` helper that introspects `docker images hexa-runner` + retains :latest + N most-recent shas would prevent accumulation. Could be run as a post-step inside build_runner_image.hexa.

constraints_honored.hexa_only: Only hexa-as-build-driver was used (./hexa run tool/build_runner_image.hexa). Dockerfile is infrastructure — single permitted edit per session.
constraints_honored.no_linux_elf_rebuild: Used existing build/hexa_linux_arm64 (575504 bytes), build/hexa_linux_x86_64 (527160 bytes), build/hexa_interp_linux_arm64 (1687856 bytes), build/hexa_interp_linux_x86_64 (2089960 bytes) — all from prior ω-runner-4 rebake.
constraints_honored.no_git_commit: Dockerfile change uncommitted; staged for caller's review.

files_touched: /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/docker/runner/Dockerfile (added scratch bridge stages + alias indirection; replaced 4-COPY+case-RUN block with 2-COPY+install-RUN; ~30 line delta)
